title: Methods for dissolving droplet particle assembly with co-solvent, and particle assemblies obtained therefrom
abstract: Some variations provide a method of assembling a plurality of particles into particle assemblies, comprising: (a) obtaining a first fluid containing particles and a solvent for the particles; (b) obtaining a second fluid not fully miscible with the first fluid; (c) obtaining a third fluid that is a co-solvent for the first fluid and the second fluid; (d) combining the first fluid and the second fluid to generate an emulsion containing droplets of the first fluid in the second fluid; (e) adding the third fluid to the emulsion; and (f) dissolving out the solvent from the droplets into the third fluid, thereby forming particle assemblies. Some variations also provide an assembly of nanoparticles, wherein the assembly has a volume from 1 Î¼m 3 to 1 mm 3 , a packing fraction from 20% to 100%, and/or an average relative surface roughness less than 1%, wherein the assembly is not disposed on a substrate.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of assembling a plurality of particles into particle assemblies, said method comprising: (a) obtaining a first fluid containing particles and a solvent for said particles; (b) obtaining a second fluid, wherein said first fluid is not fully miscible in said second fluid; (c) obtaining a third fluid that is a co-solvent for said first fluid and said second fluid; (d) combining said first fluid and said second fluid to generate an emulsion containing a dispersed phase of droplets of said first fluid in said second fluid; (e) adding said third fluid to said emulsion; and (f) dissolving said solvent from said droplets into a mixture of said second fluid and said third fluid, thereby forming particle assemblies. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein said particles are present in said first fluid in a concentration from about 1 mg/mL to about 100 mg/mL. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein said first fluid has a solubility in said second fluid from about 0.01 vol% to about 20 vol%. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein said first fluid has a solubility in said third fluid from about 1 vol% to about 100 vol% (completely miscible). 5. The method of claim 1, wherein said second fluid has a solubility in said third fluid from about 10 vol% to about 100 vol% (completely miscible). 6. The method of claim 1, wherein said solvent in said first fluid is selected from the group consisting of water, dimethyl formamide, diethyl formamide, acetonitrile, dimethyl sulfoxide, methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, 1 -propanol, isobutanol, 1 -butanol, 2-butanol, t-butanol, acetone, tetrahydrofuran, and combinations thereof. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein said second fluid is selected from the group consisting of methyl laurate, 1 -butanol, t-butanol, l-octanol, l-hexanol, l-decanol, ethyl ether, dibutyl ether, dihexyl ether, dioctyl ether, methyl t-butyl ether, methyl ethyl ketone, cyclohexane, ethyl acetate, and combinations thereof. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein said third fluid is selected from the group consisting of l-octanol, 1 -butanol, t-butanol, l-hexanol, l-decanol, ethyl ether, dibutyl ether, dihexyl ether, dioctyl ether, methyl t-butyl ether, methyl ethyl ketone, cyclohexane, ethyl acetate, and combinations thereof. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein an interface-stabilization substance is contained within said first fluid and/or said second fluid. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein said particles contain a starting ligand, and wherein said particles are ligand-exchanged to render said particles soluble in said first fluid, before or during step (a). 11. The method of claim 10, wherein said particles are ligand-exchanged to replace said starting ligand with an inorganic ligand selected from the group consisting of thiocyanate, borofluoride, hexafluorophosphate, and combinations thereof. 12. The method of claim 1, said method further comprising adjusting pH of said first fluid, prior to step (d). 13. The method of claim 1, wherein step (d) includes a step selected from the group consisting of vortex mixing, ultrasonic mixing, impeller mixing, microfluidizing, microfluidics droplet generation, porous-glass droplet generation, and combinations thereof. 14. The method of claim 1, wherein in step (f), said droplets are agitated. 15. The method of claim 1, wherein step (f) is conducted for a dissolving time from about 1 second to about 10 hours. 16. The method of claim 1, wherein step (f) is conducted at a dissolving temperature from about -l5Â°C to about l50Â°C. 17. The method of claim 1, wherein steps (d), (e), and (f) are continuous. 18. The method of claim 1, wherein steps (d), (e), and (f) are conducted in batch. 19. The method of claim 1, wherein in step (f), the number of said particle assemblies equals the number of said droplets. 20. The method of claim 1, wherein in step (f), the number of said particle assemblies is less than the number of said droplets. 21. An assembly of nanoparticles, wherein said assembly has a volume from 1 pm 3 to 1 mm 3 , a packing fraction from 20% to 100%, and an average relative surface roughness less than 5%, wherein said assembly is spherical, and wherein said assembly is not disposed on a substrate. 22. The assembly of claim 21, wherein said packing fraction is at least 90%. 23. The assembly of claim 21, wherein said average relative surface roughness is less than 0.1%. 24. The assembly of claim 21, wherein said assembly is a core-shell assembly. 25. The assembly of claim 21, wherein said assembly contains a material selected from the group consisting of metals, metal oxides, metal fluorides, metal sulfides, metal phosphides, ceramics, glasses, polymers, and combinations thereof. 26. The assembly of claim 21, wherein said assembly is free of organic ligands. 27. The assembly of claim 21, wherein said assembly consists essentially of lead(II) sulfide nanoparticles.
